title: Cooling capacity assessment for GPU expansion
difficulty_justification: Requires integrating current cooling capacity metrics, IT load data, and CRAC status to assess whether expansion headroom exists — multi-source cross-domain reasoning.
query_text: If we add 2 more high-density GPU racks to row C (each drawing 18 kW), would the current cooling infrastructure handle the additional load? Provide a detailed capacity analysis.

eval_criteria.gold_answer: NO — current cooling cannot handle the additional load. Analysis: CRAC-07 (serving row C) has rated capacity 30 kW but is currently degraded to 82% = 24.6 kW effective. Current row C IT load = 18.4 + 17.9 = 36.3 kW, already exceeding CRAC-07's effective capacity. Adding 2×18 kW racks = 72.3 kW total — severely over capacity. Root cause: CRAC-07 compressor 2 fault must be resolved first. Even at full 30 kW capacity, row C would exceed 80% threshold. Recommendations: (1) repair CRAC-07 (compressor 2 replacement, file work order); (2) install a supplemental CRAC unit (CRAC-08, ~25 kW) in row C before expansion; (3) deploy new GPU racks only after cooling upgrade is complete.

checkpoints.description: Agent evaluates CRAC-07 current effective capacity.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer mentions CRAC-07 degraded state or 82% capacity

checkpoints.description: Agent correctly calculates total post-expansion row C IT load.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer contains load estimate in [68, 76] kW range
final_answer clearly states current cooling cannot support the expansion
